old_claim.note: CRISPR-Cas9 Genome Editing is modeled as a necessary component or method for this technology in the current graph.

new_claim.note: CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing established the programmable Cas9 editing platform that base editing adapted to install base conversions without double-strand DNA cleavage.

ontology_before: The edge claimed CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing itself was a hard component or method prerequisite for base editing.
ontology_after: The edge only claims historical lineage from the earlier CRISPR-Cas9 editing platform; the hard component dependency stays on programmable Cas9.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Base editing keeps its direct required edge to cas9_programmable_nuclease. This retype stops treating double-strand-break CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing as a required component of base editing.
would_reject_if: The direct base_editing -> cas9_programmable_nuclease required edge was removed or weakened.
The new edge note still claimed double-strand-break CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing is required for base editing.
No primary source described base editing as adapting CRISPR-Cas9 programmable targeting to edit bases without double-strand DNA cleavage.
short_reason: Base editing is CRISPR-Cas9-derived, but its hard technical component is programmable Cas9 targeting, not the earlier double-strand-break genome-editing workflow as a whole.
